title: The Clockwork Rabbi
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In 16th century Prague a rabbi creates a golem to protect the Jewish quarter but the clockwork creature develops a soul.
long_description:
Prague, 1592. The Holy Roman Empire festers with religious violence. Blood libels threaten the Jewish ghetto. Rabbi Judah Loew ben Bezalel, the Maharal of Prague, has spent a lifetime studying the Kabbalistic secrets of creation. Now he must use them.

Working in the attic of the Altneuschul, Loew shapes a creature of clay and clockwork, animating it with the sacred Name of God. The Golem is meant to be a protector, a weapon of last resort. But something goes wrong in the forging. The creature awakens with a question: "Why?"

As Emperor Rudolf II, obsessed with alchemy and the occult, sets his sights on the Golem, and the Vatican sends an inquisitor to destroy it, Rabbi Loew must confront a terrible truth. He did not build a weapon. He built a son. And a son cannot be owned.

The Clockwork Rabbi is a story of faith and fatherhood, of souls in clay and gears, of the questions that arise when creation exceeds the creator's intent.
